front: Figure: led the Communist Yugoslav Partisans in World War II, then ruled Yugoslavia as dictator/President for Life until his death in 1980
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: kept his country out of the Warsaw Pact, pursuing an independent socialist foreign policy
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Figure: some conspiracy theorists claim the real Tito died and was replaced by a native Russian speaker, though mainstream scholarship holds this founder of the Yugoslav Partisans was genuinely Croatian
work: General / Giveaway Identifiers

front: Event: formed the Main Operational Group to win the Fall Weiss (Case White) offensive, a major German-led effort to destroy the Partisans
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Battle: at this engagement ("Battle of the Wounded"), the target of German Operation Knight's Move defeated a much larger force by blowing up all the bridges over the Neretva River
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Event: merged his Partisan government with the government-in-exile of King Peter II via this 1944 agreement, aiding his rise to power
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Event: after the war, put rival royalist Chetnik leader Draza Mihailovic on trial for collaboration
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Event: launched what he called the "leftist errors" while fighting Mihailovic's Chetniks for control of the town of Uzice
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Event: survived a German attempt to capture him in the Raid on Drvar, carried out by commando Otto Skorzeny as part of Operation Knight's Leap
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Event: Terence Atherton's expedition mysteriously disappeared after contacting his Partisan forces during Operation Hydra
work: World War II & the Yugoslav Partisans

front: Event: this 1948 Yugoslav-Soviet split was formalized by a Cominform resolution accusing the Yugoslav Communist Party of breaking from Marxist-Leninism and expelling Yugoslavia from that body
work: Break with Stalin & the Informbiro Period

front: Event: this split with Stalin sparked a seven-year period during which Tito imprisoned political opponents at the Goli Otok camp
work: Break with Stalin & the Informbiro Period

front: Event: grew angry with Stalin over Stalin's impolitic involvement in the Greek Civil War
work: Break with Stalin & the Informbiro Period

front: Event: Stalin's disapproval of his ambitions to annex Trieste and unite Yugoslavia with Bulgaria (via the Lake Bled accord) contributed to their break
work: Break with Stalin & the Informbiro Period

front: Figure: reportedly wrote to Stalin, "Stop sending people to kill me... If you don't stop sending killers, I'll send a very fast working one to Moscow"
work: Break with Stalin & the Informbiro Period

front: Event: elsewhere in the Eastern Bloc, alleged sympathizers with his "doctrine" (Titoism) were purged, including Hungary's Laszlo Rajk and Bulgaria's Traicho Kostov, both executed
work: Break with Stalin & the Informbiro Period

front: Event: co-founded this movement with Gamal Abdel Nasser, Jawaharlal Nehru, and Kwame Nkrumah, following the 1955 Bandung Conference
work: Founding the Non-Aligned Movement

front: Figure: served as the first Secretary-General of the Non-Aligned Movement (from 1961), its only European co-founder
work: Founding the Non-Aligned Movement

front: Figure: founded the OZNA security agency, which arrested a Borba newspaper editor for criticizing the theory of workers' self-management
work: Leadership of Socialist Yugoslavia

front: Figure: promoted the motto "Brotherhood and Unity" among Yugoslavia's constituent peoples
work: Leadership of Socialist Yugoslavia

front: Figure: was excommunicated by Pope Pius XII after convicting Archbishop Aloysius Stepinac of collaborating with the fascist Ustase regime
work: Leadership of Socialist Yugoslavia

front: Figure: his 1980 death allowed the flourishing of "Goli Otok literature" exposing the earlier Informbiro-era prison camp
work: Legacy & Death
